success_criteria.criterion: buildDeleteShotGroupMutation is moved into a dedicated pure shot-group-commands module and TimelineEditor imports it from there
Pinned shot-group membership and mode transitions are built through pure command helpers rather than ad hoc mutation assembly in multiple UI hooks
Final-video shot-group operations still preserve current external behavior after the refactor
The refactor adds or updates focused coverage for updateToLatestVideo and preserves useExternalDrop pinned-group behavior
Focused video-editor tests covering TimelineEditor, useTimelineCommit, and pinned-group behavior pass after the refactor
The full src/tools/video-editor test suite passes after the refactor
shot-group-commands module has no React dependencies
No new TimelineEditMutation variants are introduced
Manual smoke test: delete a shot via the context menu and undo it in the video editor

assumptions: The concrete delete/undo regression was caused by a split two-commit delete path, not by a proven sync race.
The existing mutation protocol (`rows`, `config`, `pinnedShotGroups`) is sufficient; the structural problem is command construction, not the commit engine.
Geometry-preserving group moves in drag and track-management code should remain outside this refactor unless repository evidence shows they need the same abstraction immediately.
Final-video mode switches are part of the same command family because they replace group membership and mode together.
No sync-suppression mechanism should be introduced unless fresh repository evidence demonstrates a real post-undo overwrite case.
